title: Cleanup handling of podman mount/unmount
body:
We should default to the user name unmount rather then the internal
name of umount.

Also User namespace was not being handled correctly. We want to inform
the user that if they do a mount when in rootless mode that they have
to be first in the podman unshare state.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: This is a back port from upstream

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.0/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I just built master and tried to `podman mount` in a `podman unshare` shell (both obviously as rootless) and got:
```Error: cannot run command "podman mount" in rootless mode```

I am pretty certain that this is still broken and these patches will not improve the situation.

Nevermind. I'm an idiot - used the wrong Podman binary.

LGTM, but CI is red.
